options.content:
The **spin-up / spin-down** half of the control plane, the counterpart to the serving dashboard. Provisioning a GPU pod is a multi-minute cold start, and "my deploy is slow" is unanswerable without splitting that wait. The deploy observer derives these from the executor's phase stream:

- **scheduling** — RunPod finding capacity for the SKU set.
- **image-pull** — host assigned, pulling the multi-GB engine image.
- **engine-init** — container up, downloading the model from HF and loading weights + CUDA init.

The *time by phase* panel is the one to read first: it attributes the cold start to the stage actually costing you. Per-deploy traces (root span `deployment.provision` with a child span per phase) sit in Tempo for the same story request-by-request. Teardown + reaper cover spin-down.

panels.title: Provision time by phase (mean seconds, stacked)
panels.description: Mean wall-clock seconds a deploy spends in each lifecycle phase, stacked so the bars sum to the mean end-to-end provision time. This is the 'where does the time go' panel: a tall image-pull band means cold image cache; a tall engine-init band means model download + weight load dominate. Means are additive (unlike percentiles), so stacking is honest here.

panels.title: End-to-end provision duration (p50 / p95)
panels.description: Wall-clock from create-pod to engine serving, across all providers/classes. The top-line 'how long does a deploy take' number. p95 is the tail an operator waits through on a cold host with an uncached image + model.

panels.title: Phase duration p95 (per phase)
panels.description: The tail of each phase separately. Complements the stacked mean panel: a phase whose mean is small but p95 is large is bimodal (e.g. image-pull that's instant on a warm host, minutes on a cold one). One line per phase.

panels.title: Provision outcomes (per result)
panels.description: Rate of provision attempts by terminal result -- running (success), timeout (hit the engine-ready deadline; the dominant cold-start failure), failed (provider/validation error). Rising timeout means capacity or image/model pulls are outrunning the deadline.

panels.title: Teardown duration (p50 / p95)
panels.description: Wall-clock for a deployment teardown (destroy through terminated), per provider. The spin-down half. Slow teardowns leak billed GPU time, so this pairs with the reaper panel to show idle cleanup is actually completing.

panels.title: Idle reaper destroys (spin-down)
panels.description: Deployments the idle-TTL reaper tore down, by reason. Operator-facing signal that leak protection is working: pods that went idle past their TTL are being reclaimed instead of billing indefinitely. Pairs with teardown duration above.
